# Requirements Quality Checklist: Phase 6 - DI Registration Cleanup

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, consistency, and completeness of Phase 6 requirements before or during review.
**Created**: 2026-06-07
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on Phase 6 feature context and requirements. It tests the written requirements, not the implementation.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are application-owned registration responsibilities fully specified, including the intentionally empty application entry point case? [Completeness, Spec FR-001, Plan Technical Approach 1]
- [ ] CHK002 Are infrastructure-owned registration responsibilities fully specified for persistence, identity stores, repositories, time helpers, rule helpers, unit-of-work boundaries, session validation, and infrastructure-backed service implementations? [Completeness, Spec FR-002, Plan Technical Approach 2]
- [ ] CHK003 Are host-owned startup responsibilities fully specified so request-pipeline concerns remain distinct from moved dependency registrations? [Completeness, Spec FR-004, Plan Technical Approach 3]
- [ ] CHK004 Are behavior-preservation requirements complete for routes, request bodies, response bodies, pagination envelopes, cookies, claims, status codes, and structured error payloads? [Completeness, Spec FR-005]
- [ ] CHK005 Are Phase 5 business-rule preservation requirements complete enough to prevent accidental vacation, employee, department, trip, and session-behavior regressions? [Completeness, Spec FR-006, Plan Dependencies]
- [ ] CHK006 Are no-schema-change requirements complete enough to exclude new tables, columns, constraints, seed data, migrations, and database resets? [Completeness, Spec FR-007, Spec FR-008]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "registration entry point" defined clearly enough that reviewers can distinguish one owning-layer entry point from ad hoc scattered registration methods? [Clarity, Spec Boundary Definitions, Spec FR-001, Spec FR-002]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "startup surface" defined clearly enough to determine which responsibilities may remain in startup composition? [Clarity, Spec Boundary Definitions, Spec FR-003, Spec FR-004]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the clarified ownership decision clear that application exposes its registration entry point while infrastructure registers infrastructure-backed implementations? [Clarity, Spec Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "runtime behavior" defined with enough specificity to avoid accidental changes to authentication, serialization, CORS, middleware order, and structured errors? [Clarity, Spec Boundary Definitions, Spec FR-005]
- [ ] CHK011 Is the local database requirement clear that Phase 5 migration readiness is a prerequisite for smoke validation, while Phase 6 itself makes no database change? [Clarity, Spec Assumptions, Plan Local Database Smoke Validation]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Do the spec and plan consistently describe Phase 6 as wiring-only with no runtime behavior change? [Consistency, Spec Scope, Plan Summary]
- [ ] CHK013 Do the spec, plan, and tasks consistently keep cookie authentication settings and events host-owned rather than moving them into lower layers? [Consistency, Spec FR-004, Plan Technical Approach 2, Tasks T022]
- [ ] CHK014 Do the spec and plan consistently avoid moving infrastructure-backed implementations into `HR.Application`? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications, Spec Edge Cases, Plan Out of Scope]
- [ ] CHK015 Do the no-migration requirements align consistently across spec, plan, quickstart expectations, and task wording? [Consistency, Spec FR-007, Plan Data Model Changes, Tasks T030]
- [ ] CHK016 Do static-check requirements consistently support architectural ownership without creating hidden runtime behavior requirements? [Consistency, Spec FR-012, Plan Static Checks, Tasks T028]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK017 Can startup readability be objectively assessed from the user story, success criteria, and ownership definitions? [Measurability, Spec User Story 1, Spec SC-003]
- [ ] CHK018 Can behavior preservation be objectively assessed from the listed compatibility dimensions and representative workflow categories? [Measurability, Spec User Story 2, Spec SC-002]
- [ ] CHK019 Is "zero direct application or infrastructure service registrations" scoped clearly enough to avoid over-broad interpretation outside the startup surface? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-004]
- [ ] CHK020 Is "zero new database migrations or schema changes" measurable from documented artifacts and repository scope? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-005, Plan Static Checks]
- [ ] CHK021 Is local database startup success tied to a clear prerequisite state rather than an undefined environment assumption? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-006, Spec Assumptions]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK022 Are maintainer readability, HR runtime preservation, and reviewer auditability each represented by independently testable user stories? [Coverage, Spec User Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK023 Are future dependency additions covered so ownership remains clear after the immediate cleanup? [Coverage, Spec User Story 1]
- [ ] CHK024 Are expected failure flows covered, including business-rule and compatibility errors, not only successful requests? [Coverage, Spec User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK025 Are application, infrastructure, and host-owned concerns all covered by reviewer-facing audit scenarios? [Coverage, Spec User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK026 Are manual smoke-validation workflow categories specified without expanding Phase 6 into new feature behavior? [Coverage, Spec SC-002, Plan Local Database Smoke Validation]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK027 Are requirements explicit that Phase 6 must not introduce Phase 7 features, role-based access changes, attendance, salary, documents, dashboard, audit logs, or new authorization behavior? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK028 Are requirements explicit that Phase 6 must not move business rules into startup composition or controllers? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK029 Are requirements explicit that Phase 6 must not reintroduce direct data-access dependencies into service classes? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK030 Are requirements explicit about preserving session rejection for terminated or soft-deleted employees after registration movement? [Edge Case, Plan Validation and Error Handling]
- [ ] CHK031 Are requirements explicit about failing clearly when required configuration is missing without changing the configured database target? [Edge Case, Spec FR-008, Plan Validation and Error Handling]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK032 Are dependencies on Phases 0 through 5 documented with enough detail to explain why Phase 6 must follow repository, business-rule, and migration-readiness work? [Dependency, Plan Dependencies on Previous Phases]
- [ ] CHK033 Are assumptions about existing session authentication and compatibility error codes documented so the cleanup cannot normalize them accidentally? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK034 Are test-provider assumptions documented clearly enough to separate production SQL Server registration from automated SQLite test composition? [Assumption, Plan Technical Approach 4]
- [ ] CHK035 Are local environment notes directed to handoff or completion artifacts instead of turning `tasks.md` into a runtime failure log? [Assumption, Tasks T001, Tasks T034]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK036 Does the specification avoid ambiguity between application-owned contracts and infrastructure-owned implementation registrations? [Ambiguity, Spec Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK037 Does the plan avoid conflict between moving `ApplicationDbContext` registration into infrastructure and keeping host-owned authentication cookie events in startup composition? [Conflict, Plan Technical Approach 2, Plan Technical Approach 3]
- [ ] CHK038 Does the checklist/readiness documentation avoid treating implementation verification as a substitute for requirements clarity? [Ambiguity, Existing Checklist requirements.md, Existing Checklist di-registration.md]
- [ ] CHK039 Does the Phase 6 documentation avoid conflict with Phase 7 by explicitly excluding new advanced HR domains? [Conflict, Spec Edge Cases, Plan Out of Scope]
- [ ] CHK040 Are all terms that carry architecture meaning, including "owning layer", "startup surface", "registration entry point", and "runtime behavior", defined or traceable to definitions? [Ambiguity, Spec Boundary Definitions]

## Notes

- This checklist validates Phase 6 requirements quality only.
- It does not verify source code behavior, database state, migration output, or automated test results.
- Keep items unchecked until a reviewer has evaluated the written Phase 6 artifacts.
